description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a triplane wing configuration, featuring three stacked wings with roundels in blue, white, and red on the upper surfaces. The fuselage is painted in a light tan color with a circular insignia on the side, and the tail section is striped in blue, yellow, and red. The nose houses a visible propeller and radial engine, while the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, typical of early 20th-century fighter aircraft. The overall design is reminiscent of World War I-era planes, with a compact, robust build and colorful markings.
description: A vintage triplane with colorful roundels, open cockpit, and a visible front propeller, reminiscent of early 20th-century fighter aircraft.
description_view_0: Side view showing the tan fuselage, colorful tail, and stacked wings with roundels.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail's striped pattern and the triplane wing arrangement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the fuselage insignia, tail colors, and wing structure.
description_view_3: Front view featuring the radial engine, propeller, and the three stacked wings with roundels.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
